title: Train a censored time-to-next-pit model and convert it to P(stop within 1 lap)
summary: This target is naturally a time-to-event problem with right censoring at race end or current stint continuation.
rationale: This target is naturally a time-to-event problem with right censoring at race end or current stint continuation. XGBoost’s AFT survival objective gives a distinct model family from the current binary booster and may capture smooth remaining-laps structure better than more hand-built thresholds.
implementation_hint: For each row, define remaining laps until the next observed PitStop for that driver-race sequence; right-censor rows with no future stop. Fit an AFT model on engineered features, then convert the predicted survival distribution into Pr(T<=1 lap). Blend its OOF ranks with the binary classifier rather than replacing the baseline outright.
expected_effect: Adds a genuinely different inductive bias and may especially help rows far from or near the stop boundary where binary labels are noisy snapshots of a longer process.
risk: Constructing the censored target must be strictly forward-looking within each sequence; AFT misspecification can underperform if stop timing is highly multimodal by regime.
